rationale: Both candidates independently find the same valid async_chunk default (True vs False) inconsistency and correctly validate that the seq_len nit was already fixed and the streaming scoping was already resolved. Y goes further: it explicitly flags that the split now depends solely on total_num_scheduled_tokens and notes the risk for tensors keyed on hidden_states.shape[0] instead — directly touching the dual-batch-axis gap that PR #4910 later fixed — while X's extra findings (NPU runner parity, missing test assertion) are plausible but more speculative/lower-value. X is slightly more actionable per-finding (concrete fix asks), but Y's deeper control-flow verification and gap coverage edge it out.
